_comment: Feature narrative deck for the slidey-edit spatial-annotation capability. Paths, semantic refs, bbox, and the edited-element ref are replayed verbatim from stories/slidey-edit/cassettes/deck_review.cassette.yaml (host.agent.task author_deck, host.slidey.render, host.starlark.run seed_annotation). The embed:view/annotate/pick protocol is documented in docs/architecture (kitsoki host ⟷ slidey producer).

scenes.title: Point at a slide, ask for a change
scenes.subtitle: Author a deck, then refine a single element by spatial reference
scenes.narration: Slidey-edit lets you author a deck and then refine one element of it — not by editing JSON by hand, but by pointing at the rendered slide and describing the change you want.

scenes.lede: Decks are JSON; feedback is spatial.
scenes.body: A rendered deck is a wall of scenes, but the change you want is local: 'make that card stand out.' Mapping a visual intent back to the right scene and element in the source by hand is slow and error-prone. The feature closes that loop — the rendered slide carries machine-addressable handles, so a spatial pick becomes a precise edit.
scenes.narration: A deck is JSON, but feedback is spatial — make that card stand out. The feature maps a visual pick back to the exact element in the source.

scenes.title: How it works
scenes.narration: Three host calls compose the loop: an agent authors the deck, the renderer emits a semantic map alongside the HTML, and a glue script seeds an annotation anchored to a real element.

scenes.title: The semantic anchor — addressable by ref + bbox
scenes.caption: The renderer's deck.semantic.json gives every element an opaque ref and a bounding box, so a click on the slide resolves to a precise edit target — no JSON spelunking.

scenes.body: With the element picked, the refine instruction — 'make the semantic_element card stand out and add a one-line example' — flows to the authoring agent. It returns the edited spec and reports exactly which refs it touched: edited = ['1/card_0']. Nothing else in the deck moves.
scenes.narration: With the card picked, the instruction flows to the agent. It returns the edited deck and reports exactly one ref touched — card zero on scene one. Nothing else moves.

scenes.body: The host speaks a generic embed protocol — view, annotate, pick — over postMessage. Slidey is the producer on the other side; kitsoki never learns slidey's scene vocabulary, and the story owns the scene gate. New producers plug in behind the same protocol without touching the runtime.
scenes.narration: The host speaks a generic embed protocol — view, annotate, pick. Slidey is just the producer behind it. Kitsoki never learns slidey's vocabulary, so new producers plug in unchanged.

scenes.title: Try it
scenes.subtitle: kitsoki web → drive the slidey-edit story → author, pick, refine
scenes.narration: See it live — drive the slidey-edit story, author a deck, point at a card, and refine it.
